perturbation.original_text: 11. This Agreement shall be binding during the Purpose and shall remain in effect for a period or 2 years after the Purpose is completed.
perturbation.changed_text: 11. This Agreement shall be binding during the Purpose and shall remain in effect until either party provides 30 days written notice of termination, regardless of the Purpose's completion.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction. Originally, the agreement lasts for a fixed period (2 years after the Purpose). Now, it can be terminated with 30 days' notice, creating uncertainty about the agreement's duration. This contradicts the original fixed term agreement, as now either party can terminate at will regardless of the 2-year term, and the completion of the Purpose.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 11. This Agreement shall be binding during the Purpose and shall remain in effect until either party provides 30 days written notice of termination, regardless of the Purpose's completion.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a contradiction. The original text specifies a duration of 2 years after the Purpose is completed. The changed text allows termination with 30 days' notice, regardless of the Purpose's completion. This directly conflicts with the original fixed term agreement and creates uncertainty about the agreement's duration. Because the Changed Text and Contradicted Text are the same in this case, that doesn't mean there isn't a contradiction. The explanation clearly highlights how a contradiction exists, thus following Perturbation Type: Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 3. The Receiving Party shall protect the Disclosing Party’s inter est in the Confidential Information and shall keep the Confidential Information confidential. The Receiving Party shall not disclose, allow access to, transmit or transfer the Confidential Information to any third party without the Disclosing Party’s prior written consent, provided however that the Receiving Party may disclose the Confidential Information to those of its directors, officers and employees ("Representatives") who have a need to know the Confidential Information for the Purpose provided that the Receiving Party shall ensure that such Representatives treat the Confidential Information as confidential and at all times in a manner consistent with this Agreement and the Receiving Party shall be liable for any loss or damage resulting from any Repre sentative failing to do so.
perturbation.changed_text: 3. The Receiving Party shall protect the Disclosing Party’s inter est in the Confidential Information and shall keep the Confidential Information confidential. The Receiving Party shall not disclose, allow access to, transmit or transfer the Confidential Information to any third party without the Disclosing Party’s prior written consent, provided however that the Receiving Party may disclose the Confidential Information to those of its directors, officers, employees, and consultants ("Representatives") who have a need to know the Confidential Information for any business purpose, provided that the Receiving Party shall ensure that such Representatives treat the Confidential Information as confidential and at all times in a manner consistent with this Agreement. The Receiving Party shall not be liable for any loss or damage resulting from any Representative failing to do so.
perturbation.explanation: The original text limits disclosure to representatives with a 'need to know the Confidential Information for the Purpose' of the agreement. The changed text broadens this to 'any business purpose'. Also the last sentance was changed to state that they will not be liable for damage or loss. This creates an in-text contradiction because representatives can now access the information for reasons beyond the original 'Purpose', contradicting the restrictions of Clause 2 and the obligation to keep confidential information for the original defined purpose. It also contradicts the prior text in the same clause that states they will be liable for any damage or loss.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2. The Receiving Party shall use the Confidential Information solely for the Pur pose and shall not use the Confidential Information in any manner except as reasonably required for the Purpose. Nothing in this Agreement obligates the Disclosing Party to make any particular disclosure of Confidential Information. This Agreement shall ap ply to all Confidential Information regardless of its form or medium, whether conveyed orally, visually, electronically or in writing, and whether or not it is designated as "confidential".
perturbation.justification: The changed text broadens the scope of permissible use of confidential information by representatives from 'the Purpose' to 'any business purpose'. This directly contradicts Clause 2, which explicitly states that the Receiving Party shall use the Confidential Information 'solely for the Purpose' and 'shall not use the Confidential Information in any manner except as reasonably required for the Purpose'. Additionally, the removal of liability for representatives failing to treat the information as confidential contradicts the original text within the same clause where the Receiving Party was liable for such failures.

perturbation.original_text: 6. The Confidential Information shall not be copied or reproduced in any form or stored in a retrieval system or database by the Receiv ing Party without the prior consent of the Disclosing Party except for such copies and storage as may reasonably be required internally by the Receiving Party for the Purpose. All copies of the Confidential Information shall contain the same proprietary n otices which may appear on the original.
perturbation.changed_text: 6. The Confidential Information may be copied or reproduced in any form or stored in a retrieval system or database by the Receiving Party without the prior consent of the Disclosing Party if such copying, reproduction, or storage is in compliance with standard industry practices. All copies of the Confidential Information shall contain the same proprietary n otices which may appear on the original.
perturbation.explanation: The original text strictly prohibits copying or reproducing Confidential Information without prior consent, only allowing it for internal needs 'for the Purpose'. The changed text allows it if it's 'in compliance with standard industry practices', even without consent. This creates a direct in-text contradiction regarding whether consent is required. This also contradicts clause 2, which the information is to be used only for the purpose.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2. The Receiving Party shall use the Confidential Information solely for the Pur pose and shall not use the Confidential Information in any manner except as reasonably required for the Purpose. Nothing in this Agreement obligates the Disclosing Party to make any particular disclosure of Confidential Information. This Agreement shall ap ply to all Confidential Information regardless of its form or medium, whether conveyed orally, visually, electronically or in writing, and whether or not it is designated as "confidential".
perturbation.justification: The changed text in clause 6 allows copying and reproduction of confidential information based on 'standard industry practices' without the Disclosing Party's consent. This directly contradicts clause 2, which states the Receiving Party shall use the Confidential Information 'solely for the Purpose' and 'shall not use the Confidential Information in any manner except as reasonably required for the Purpose.' Clause 6 now creates an exception not present in Clause 2, establishing a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 7. The Receiving Party shall upon request immediately return to the Disclosing Party or destroy, as directed by the Disclosing Party , the Confidential Information and all copies thereof in any form whatsoever under the power or control of the Receiving Party or its Representatives and immediately destroy all compilations, analysis or derivative work relating to the Confidential Information. Where the Receiving Party satisfies its obligation to return or destroy the embodiments of the Confidential Information by destroying such embodiments, the Receiving Party will deliver to the Disclosing Party a certificate signed by an officer of the Receiving Party certifying such destruction of such embodiments .
perturbation.changed_text: 7. The Receiving Party shall upon request immediately return to the Disclosing Party or destroy, as directed by the Disclosing Party , the Confidential Information and all copies thereof in any form whatsoever under the power or control of the Receiving Party or its Representatives except to the extent such copies are retained in archived backups in accordance with the Receiving Party’s ordinary data retention policies. Where the Receiving Party satisfies its obligation to return or destroy the embodiments of the Confidential Information by destroying such embodiments, the Receiving Party will deliver to the Disclosing Party a certificate signed by an officer of the Receiving Party certifying such destruction of such embodiments .
perturbation.explanation: The original text requires immediate return or destruction of all Confidential Information upon request. The changed text adds an exception: copies retained in archived backups according to the Receiving Party's data retention policies. This creates an in-text contradiction, as the 'immediate return or destroy' obligation is now undermined by the exception. This contradicts the clear requirements, opening to disputes over what is considered acceptable data retention.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 7. The Receiving Party shall upon request immediately return to the Disclosing Party or destroy, as directed by the Disclosing Party , the Confidential Information and all copies thereof in any form whatsoever under the power or control of the Receiving Party or its Representatives except to the extent such copies are retained in archived backups in accordance with the Receiving Party’s ordinary data retention policies. Where the Receiving Party satisfies its obligation to return or destroy the embodiments of the Confidential Information by destroying such embodiments, the Receiving Party will deliver to the Disclosing Party a certificate signed by an officer of the Receiving Party certifying such destruction of such embodiments .
perturbation.justification: The original text requires immediate return or destruction of all Confidential Information. The changed text introduces an exception for archived backups, which contradicts the 'immediate return or destroy' obligation. The contradicted text (which is the same as the changed text) now contains conflicting information because the clause states that the Receiving Party must immediately return or destroy all confidential information, but then also states that they do not have to return or destroy confidential information stored in archived backups in accordance with the Receiving Party’s ordinary data retention policies, thus creating a direct conflict within the same clause.

perturbation.original_text: 8. All right, t itle and interest in and to the Confidential Information, including, without limitation, all patent rights, trade secret and confidential information rights, copyrights and other intellectual property, industrial, proprietary and other rights of any kind o r nature, shall remain the exclusive property of the Disclosing Party , and the Confidential Information shall be held in trust and confidence by the Receiving Party for the Disclosing Party . The Disclosing Party shall retain title to all tangible media on which Confidential Information resides, including documentation, discs, and all copies thereof. No interest, license or any other right in, or to, the Confidential Information, other than expressly set out herein , is granted to the Receiving Party under this Agreement by implication or otherwise. Although the Confidential Information is obtained from sources believed to be reliable, the Confidential Information is provided hereunder on a strictly "as is", "where i s", "with all faults" basis, and without warranties, conditions or representations of any kind, express or implied, and this Agreement, and the provision of Confidential Information, shall not constitute any representation, warranty or guarantee to the Receiving Party by the Disclosing Party whatsoever, including without limitation any representation, warranty or guarantee with respect to the Confidential Information infringing, violating, misappropriating or otherwise interfering with any rights of third p arties. The Disclosing Party shall not be held liable for any errors or omissions in the Confidential Information or the use, or the results of the use of, the Confidential Information. The Disclosing Party shall have no responsibility for updating or correcting any Confidential Information provided hereunder.
perturbation.changed_text: 8. All right, t itle and interest in and to the Confidential Information, including, without limitation, all patent rights, trade secret and confidential information rights, copyrights and other intellectual property, industrial, proprietary and other rights of any kind o r nature, shall remain the exclusive property of the Disclosing Party , and the Confidential Information shall be held in trust and confidence by the Receiving Party for the Disclosing Party , except for any improvements or derivative works created by the Receiving Party based on the Confidential Information, for which the Receiving Party shall own the rights. The Disclosing Party shall retain title to all tangible media on which Confidential Information resides, including documentation, discs, and all copies thereof. No interest, license or any other right in, or to, the Confidential Information, other than expressly set out herein , is granted to the Receiving Party under this Agreement by implication or otherwise. Although the Confidential Information is obtained from sources believed to be reliable, the Confidential Information is provided hereunder on a strictly "as is", "where i s", "with all faults" basis, and without warranties, conditions or representations of any kind, express or implied, and this Agreement, and the provision of Confidential Information, shall not constitute any representation, warranty or guarantee to the Receiving Party by the Disclosing Party whatsoever, including without limitation any representation, warranty or guarantee with respect to the Confidential Information infringing, violating, misappropriating or otherwise interfering with any rights of third p arties. The Disclosing Party shall not be held liable for any errors or omissions in the Confidential Information or the use, or the results of the use of, the Confidential Information. The Disclosing Party shall have no responsibility for updating or correcting any Confidential Information provided hereunder.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states that all rights to the Confidential Information remain the exclusive property of the Disclosing Party. The change adds an exception, giving the Receiving Party ownership of rights to improvements or derivative works based on the Confidential Information. This creates an in-text contradiction about who owns the rights to derived works. If the improvement is using the Confidential Information, this would create an in-text contradiction. The original clause states that all intellectual property shall remain of the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 8. All right, t itle and interest in and to the Confidential Information, including, without limitation, all patent rights, trade secret and confidential information rights, copyrights and other intellectual property, industrial, proprietary and other rights of any kind o r nature, shall remain the exclusive property of the Disclosing Party , and the Confidential Information shall be held in trust and confidence by the Receiving Party for the Disclosing Party , except for any improvements or derivative works created by the Receiving Party based on the Confidential Information, for which the Receiving Party shall own the rights. The Disclosing Party shall retain title to all tangible media on which Confidential Information resides, including documentation, discs, and all copies thereof. No interest, license or any other right in, or to, the Confidential Information, other than expressly set out herein , is granted to the Receiving Party under this Agreement by implication or otherwise. Although the Confidential Information is obtained from sources believed to be reliable, the Confidential Information is provided hereunder on a strictly "as is", "where i s", "with all faults" basis, and without warranties, conditions or representations of any kind, express or implied, and this Agreement, and the provision of Confidential Information, shall not constitute any representation, warranty or guarantee to the Receiving Party by the Disclosing Party whatsoever, including without limitation any representation, warranty or guarantee with respect to the Confidential Information infringing, violating, misappropriating or otherwise interfering with any rights of third p arties. The Disclosing Party shall not be held liable for any errors or omissions in the Confidential Information or the use, or the results of the use of, the Confidential Information. The Disclosing Party shall have no responsibility for updating or correcting any Confidential Information provided hereunder.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces an exception to the Disclosing Party's exclusive ownership of Confidential Information, granting the Receiving Party ownership of improvements or derivative works. This directly contradicts the initial statement that all rights, title, and interest in the Confidential Information, including all intellectual property rights, shall remain the exclusive property of the Disclosing Party. The contradicted text is the changed text itself and the initial statement in the original text, creating an in-text contradiction as it creates uncertainty about who owns the derived works.

perturbation.original_text: 9. The Receiving Party understands and agrees that the Confidential Information is information of a confidential nature of the Disclosing Party , is consistently treated as such by the Disclosing Part y and that the public release of this information shall cause irreparable damage to the Disclosing Party . The Confidential Information is being disclosed by the Disclosing Party in specific reliance on the commitments and representations of the Receiving P arty in this Agreement. It is accordingly agreed that the Disclosing Party shall be entitled to injunctive relief to prevent breaches of this Agreement and to specifically enforce the terms and provisions hereof, in addition to any other remedy to which the Disclosing Party may be entitled at law or in equity.
perturbation.changed_text: 9. The Receiving Party understands and agrees that the Confidential Information is information of a confidential nature of the Disclosing Party , is consistently treated as such by the Disclosing Part y and that the public release of this information may cause damage to the Disclosing Party. The Confidential Information is being disclosed by the Disclosing Party without specific reliance on the commitments and representations of the Receiving P arty in this Agreement. It is accordingly agreed that the Disclosing Party shall not be entitled to injunctive relief to prevent breaches of this Agreement, but may seek other remedies to which the Disclosing Party may be entitled at law or in equity.
perturbation.explanation: The original text asserts that public release of Confidential Information 'shall cause irreparable damage' and that the information is disclosed 'in specific reliance' on the Receiving Party's commitments. It also allows the Disclosing Party to seek injunctive relief. The changed text weakens the 'irreparable damage' claim to 'may cause damage', removes the 'specific reliance' assertion, and states that Disclosing party is not entitled to injunctive relief. This creates several in-text contradictions. It creates doubt about the extent of damage it can cause, also, by stating that the information isn't disclosed in reliance to the receiving party, it weakens the terms of the agreement. Also, it changes the actions that can be taken.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 9. The Receiving Party understands and agrees that the Confidential Information is information of a confidential nature of the Disclosing Party , is consistently treated as such by the Disclosing Part y and that the public release of this information may cause damage to the Disclosing Party. The Confidential Information is being disclosed by the Disclosing Party without specific reliance on the commitments and representations of the Receiving P arty in this Agreement. It is accordingly agreed that the Disclosing Party shall not be entitled to injunctive relief to prevent breaches of this Agreement, but may seek other remedies to which the Disclosing Party may be entitled at law or in equity.
perturbation.justification: The changed text significantly alters the original agreement. The original text specifies 'shall cause irreparable damage' and reliance on the Receiving Party's commitments, entitling the Disclosing Party to injunctive relief. The changed text weakens the damage claim to 'may cause damage,' removes the 'specific reliance' assertion, and explicitly states that the Disclosing Party is 'not entitled to injunctive relief.' The contradicted text is identical to the changed text, meaning that the changes made to the original now stand in contradiction to what was previously established, creating a clear in-text contradiction, especially regarding the entitlement to injunctive relief, and adherence to the definition: Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction
